title: Failed assertions in final blocks not reflected in exit code anymore
body:
Running this simulation:
```
module tb();
    initial begin
        $finish();
    end
    final begin
        assert(1 == 0);
    end
endmodule
```
results in exit code `0`, but it used to (correctly imo) exit with `1` until recently.
It started with #6891 and specifically with this line:
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/pull/6891/changes#diff-1c0f1298e2ed5505c79d957359eb3bdfee1dfa2ca6efdb67c2f9a28126639804R163

At least in this case, it appears that @wsnyder's comment in the PR applies, and the stop (from the assert) should override the finish. Is there a good way to tell when that override should happen or not?
